title: Fix --tls-verify
body:
- Make the --help presence match deprecation status
- Warn on (skopeo --tls-verify command) again

See https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1350#discussion_r667184279 for past discussion

<s>WIP, this needs unit tests and much more documentation.</s>

@edsantiago I think this works, but I’d very much appreciate a second pair of eyes.

comments.body:
I'm really sorry, I started to review this last week but it's way over my head. All I can do is compile and see what `--help` looks like, and run my man page xref on it. The latter works fine, it now suggests adding `--tls-verify` back to the man pages for `delete`, `inspect`, `list-tags`, and `login`. 

What I don't really like is the `--help` output. This isn't your fault, it's already broken in current skopeo, but:
```console
$ bin/skopeo inspect --help | grep tls
      --tls-verify                  require HTTPS and verify certificates when talking to the container registry or daemon (defaults to true) (default )
```
Note the duplicate 'default', one useful, one not. I don't know enough Cobra to know how to fix that, but mention it in case you do.

Anyhow, in principle, this LGTM. It has the desired effect of documenting `--tls-verify` in the `help` output where it belongs, and that makes me happy.

@edsantiago Thanks, that’s exactly the kind of check I was looking for.

---

> Note the duplicate 'default', one useful, one not.

That is actually my fault, as the author of `optionalBoolFlag`, and something I didn’t notice before. Let split that out to https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1383 , it’s probably fixable but needs some investigation.

This is now ready for review.
- `skopeo logout` newly accepts non-deprecated `--tls-verify`
- `skopeo manifest-digest` and the signing commands now don’t accept the `--tls-verify` subcommand option and fail (but still accept the global one with a warning)
- The visibility of `--tls-verify` in `--help`, and warning on use, should otherwise work as expected.

See individual commit messages for details.

> But since we're on it, can we tackle the empty default value `(default )`?

That’s filed separately as https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1383 ; it might be a simple change but it’s not obviously trivial. I can work on that now if you think it is a blocker.

> > But since we're on it, can we tackle the empty default value `(default )`?
> 
> That’s filed separately as #1383 ; it might be a simple change but it’s not obviously trivial. I can work on that now if you think it is a blocker.

Thanks! Not a blocker at all, let's get it in.
